segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a dramatic, heroic orchestral and electronic score accompanying a title sequence featuring the bold red 'MARVEL' logo, followed by 'RISING' emerging from a fiery, pixelated explosion, and 'PLAYING WITH FIRE' in a bold orange font. The music then softens, giving way to ambient museum sounds of light chatter and footsteps as the scene transitions to a well-lit museum exhibit titled 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS'. A diverse group of visitors, including children and adults, observe ancient artifacts displayed in glass cases, with the camera panning across relics and a large black cat statue. A girl with long black hair, dressed in a teal jacket and pink scarf, gestures animatedly while holding a smartphone, speaking energetically about how "Our ancestors thousands of years ago were working with precious metals and gemstones," and how the "artistry... tells us a lot about their culture." A boy with reddish-brown hair, in a black jacket over a red hoodie, listens with a slightly concerned expression, then asks calmly, "Are you checking here or watch again?" The girl, in a defensive and rapid-fire tone, explains she's "just staying vigilant," seeing if "there are any heroes we need help?" She then expresses frustration that "things have been boring lately," with "every villain seems to be on vacation," lamenting, "We can't do that without bad guys? Give me a J-Walker, something!" The boy, now smiling and gesturing, responds in a relaxed, content voice that he's "digging the downtime," having reached "level 10 on Cosmic Blaster" and tried "every flavor at the vegan donut shop," advising with a hint of disgust to "Skip Basil Rhubarb Crunch... weird." The girl, looking at her phone and making a dismissive gesture, reacts with disbelief and enthusiasm, declaring, "Serious? Basil Rhubarb Crunch is amazing!" as she hands the phone to the boy. The scene ends with a close-up of a woman with long dark hair and yellow-framed glasses, wearing a brown vest over a blue shirt, observing the interaction with a serious expression, all within the museum's classical architecture and natural light.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a warmly lit museum hall, showcasing ancient artifacts like a black cat statue and a golden headdress in glass display cases, under a 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS' banner. A female guide's voice instructs, "Kamala, Dante, pay attention," mentioning a potential quiz, which elicits a collective groan from the visitors. She then sarcastically notes their "enthusiasm for history" before describing the exhibit's centerpiece: a "priceless crown" of "solid gold with platinum inlays and 100 precious stones." The Girl with long black hair, wearing a teal jacket, shows a text message to the Boy with reddish-brown hair, in a black jacket, which reads: 'I'm in town. We NEED to talk.' A tense, magical sound builds as the Girl asks, "Everything okay?" and the Boy replies, "Yeah, it's fine." Suddenly, the lighting shifts to an urgent red as both transform with a whoosh and power-up sound. The Girl becomes the superheroine Ms. Marvel, in a blue and red costume with a lightning bolt emblem and red cape, excitedly exclaiming, "Woohoo! Finally, an emergency!" The Boy transforms into the superhero Inferno, in a black and red suit with a circular emblem, looking more hesitant as he suggests, "Maybe we should let museum security handle it." Ms. Marvel dismisses this with a confident "No way! Secret Warriors coming in hot!" while Inferno sheepishly adds he's "just workshopping it." A comedic struggle ensues as Ms. Marvel exclaims, "Hold on! My arm's caught in the sleeve!" followed by a grunt and a "boom" sound. Striking heroic poses, they confront a surprised Woman in a denim jacket and glasses. Ms. Marvel confidently declares, "Where's the bad guy? He doesn't stand a chance now that Ms. Marvel and Inferno are here!" The Woman, looking startled, tentatively responds, "Um, hi. I think maybe..."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a grand museum hall, labeled 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS,' where the Boy with reddish-brown hair, in his superhero costume, stands with a stern expression as the Girl with long black hair, also in her superheroine costume, points accusingly at the Woman with a denim jacket and glasses. The Woman, initially surprised, responds with a calm female voice, stating, "I just got a little too close to the display." The Boy, with a young male voice, presses, "You sure you didn't see anyone else? Maybe a villain engaged in nefarious misdeeds?" The Woman laughs, then introduces herself as "Zayla" with a calm voice, explaining, "Yeah, no. I'm Zayla, I'm a part of the field trip. Sorry." The Boy then tells her to "Catch up with the class." The Girl, with a young female voice, questions Zayla, "Who exactly are you supposed to be?" and then, "Hey, aren't you in my class?" The Boy quickly interjects, "Nah, no, no, he's no one. He's just someone out there fighting crime. You should really pay attention to the teacher. Eyes forward, young lady!" The scene transitions to an exterior shot of a brick building with a sign reading 'WILSON'S AUTO,' then inside the dim, industrial lighting of the auto shop. The Boy and Girl, now in civilian clothes, walk in and approach Zayla, who is now working on a green bicycle frame, wearing a red jacket with yellow stars and a dog tag necklace, appearing unimpressed. The Girl asks, "Do you think we're really having a quiz on the museum exhibit?" The Boy responds, "If so, cool with me. All about the normal stuff, remember?" The Girl, gesturing, declares, "I used to believe the hype about normal, but it is so overrated. My life now blows it out of the water." The Boy replies, "I'm pretty sure you were always above average." The Girl laughs and asks, "America, what do you think? Being normal? Yes or who needs it?" revealing her name. Zayla, still displaying a serious and unimpressed demeanor, responds, "I'm sorry. I wasn't listening to what..."

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in Wilson's Auto Shop, where the Boy with reddish-brown hair, dressed in a black jacket over a red hoodie, stands beside the Girl with long black hair, who wears a teal jacket and holds a pink smartphone. They appear to be in a serious conversation, punctuated by distinct text message notification sounds from the Girl's phone. The Girl, whose name is Gwen, gestures as she speaks, expressing frustration, "That's the millionth text you've gotten today. What's going on?" The Boy, with a calm tone, dismisses it as "no, no more than usual, just, you know, nothing." Gwen responds, "Yeah. Seems like nothing," followed by another text notification. The Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses, named Doreen, joins them, arms crossed and displaying a skeptical expression, while Gwen explains the texts are "just my mom. Since I joined the Secret Warriors, I keep missing family stuff. She's all over me." The Boy suggests, "it's good things are slow. You can put in some family time," to which Gwen sarcastically replies, "and have my mom question every single one of my life choices? Sorry, Yummy. Science project." The Boy questions, "That's kind of what this is, right?" Gwen then playfully reassures him, "Don't worry. Smiley face? Smiley face," adding, "Only two. Any more than that and she'll suspect something." The scene transitions as Gwen activates a device on her jacket, revealing a glowing red circular emblem with a lightning bolt, triggering a portal beneath them. They step into a futuristic, circular chamber with a red and white target design on the floor, and the portal closes. They emerge in a high-tech, multi-level recreational space with a climbing wall, basketball hoop, and a large screen displaying a game. The Boy calls out, "Hey Gwen, Doreen," as he and Gwen sit on a ledge. Doreen, lounging nearby and writing in a notebook, sighs "Oh, blebs," and later, "Great," indicating her weary or annoyed demeanor. Gwen continues to interact with her phone, showing concern, and makes a peace sign, suggesting a shift from tension to a more relaxed, playful atmosphere, while stating, "Ammy says I have to be home for dinner tomorrow. Special family."

segments.integrated_caption: The scene unfolds in a vibrant, futuristic recreational space, featuring large windows overlooking a mountainous landscape, a game-displaying screen, and a circular platform, all bathed in modern, high-tech lighting. The Girl with long black hair, dressed in a teal jacket, pink scarf, and yellow top, is initially engaged in a phone conversation, discussing 'the event' and 'three smiley faces,' which she finds 'stressful,' exclaiming, 'Why is this so stressful!' A calm female voice, belonging to the Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses, who lounges on a ledge with a serious expression, asks about the 'family event,' while another female voice, from the Woman with denim jacket and glasses (now in a red jacket with yellow stars), humorously lists symptoms. The Girl with long black hair then turns her attention to a large, sleeping orange dog with a red collar and a fork-shaped marking on its forehead, which makes distinct grunting, snorting, and stretching sounds. She addresses it as 'Mushface,' asking, 'What's wrong?' As she gently touches the dog, it lets out a loud yawn and stretch, revealing a small, green-furred squirrel with a yellow bow tie inside its mouth. The squirrel, making frantic squeaking and chittering sounds, leaps out, causing a cloud of white smoke to erupt from the dog’s mouth, startling the Girl with long black hair. The Woman with long dark hair identifies the dog as 'Lockjaw,' noting he's 'been doing that all day' and 'must have caught a bummer of a cold,' while the Woman with denim jacket adds that symptoms include 'uncontrollable teleportations,' which is visually confirmed by the dog's powerful yawn and 'poof' sound. The Boy with reddish-brown hair, wearing a black jacket over a red hoodie, appears concerned in the foreground, and a new character, the Girl with blonde hair tipped with pink, wearing a white crop top with a pink spider emblem, observes with a stern look, eventually exclaiming 'Tyne!' as the squirrel continues its frantic sounds.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a futuristic recreational space, revealing a mountainous landscape through large windows. A Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses, in a red jacket with yellow stars, stands with arms crossed, observing a large, cracked ice block, while a Girl with long black hair gestures animatedly beside her. The focus shifts to a red-haired woman in a denim jacket, who excitedly makes a peace sign while interacting with a large, grumpy-looking squirrel-like creature. The woman addresses the creature as 'Dorps' and 'Doreen,' and later 'Tippy,' asking it to 'take care of the sick puppy' and encouraging it by reminding it nursed her through food poisoning, promising 'all the nut bars' if it helps out. The creature, making chittering and squeaking sounds, initially looks annoyed but then closes its eyes in apparent satisfaction, accompanied by mechanical whirring and clanking. Suddenly, with a distinct 'thwack' and 'splat,' the creature leaps forward, growling, knocking the woman back, and both are splattered with green goo, which also covers the Girl with long black hair, who reacts with shock. Upbeat, slightly dramatic synth music begins as the scene cuts to a Boy with reddish-brown hair, sitting and observing a Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, who is perched on a ledge. The blonde girl exclaims 'Ugh' at the mess, then sarcastically suggests it's a good time to 'practice my super fast costume change' before announcing she needs to run to the music store because she 'broke a drumstick,' having 'been feeling the rhythm a little too much.' The unimpressed blonde girl then turns to face the boy as he stands up, crossing his arms, and he offers to 'come along,' noting he hasn't been to a music store in what 'feels like forever.' The environment features modern design elements, including a climbing wall, a basketball hoop, and a large screen displaying a game, under vibrant, cool-toned lighting.

You get the tissues, I'll grab the mop." The scene transitions to a brightly lit music store with red walls, records, and instruments, where the Boy, now holding a drumstick, and the Girl are behind a counter. The Boy, wearing a black jacket over a red hoodie, remarks, "These were always my favorite," prompting the Girl, in a white crop top with a pink spider emblem, to exclaim with excitement, "Oh no way. As a melancholic tune begins, the Boy leans in earnestly, explaining that he and friends in Illinois were "gonna start a band" but he "haven't played since I lost my drums in the fire," adding, "But I'm—I'm cool just hanging out in the music store. The Girl, initially smirking, responds with sarcasm, "Um, since when are you not normal?" to which the Boy shockingly reveals, "I don't know, maybe since I burned down my family's house. Destroyed everything they had." The Girl's expression shifts as she acknowledges, "Oh. Yeah, you're right. You're definitely not normal." She then shares her own trauma, "Well, you know, my dad literally hunted me across the city because he thought the ghost spider killed my best friend." The Boy acknowledges her with a simple "Right," and the Girl concludes, "I guess it's an understatement that family stuff can be hard for people like us, huh?" Throughout this intense conversation, the Boy pulls out a smartphone, revealing a text message that reads 'I REALLY need to see you,' causing the Girl’s expression to shift to concern and the Boy’s face to show a mix of urgency and worry, all under consistent bright lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a brightly lit music store with red walls, where a Boy with reddish-brown hair, wearing a black jacket over a red hoodie, tells a Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, in a white crop top with a pink spider emblem, that he's "gonna take a walk" to "clear my head." The Girl playfully asks if he'll "come play with us sometime," to which he hesitantly replies, "Yeah, sure," before a distinct thump sounds. The scene then shifts to a dark, high-tech museum at night, accompanied by a mysterious electronic melody and the hum of security systems. Red laser grids crisscross the hall as a Security guard in a blue uniform scans the area with a flashlight. A Woman with dark skin, wearing a black sleeveless top, approaches with a confident smirk, her hand crackling with blue energy, which audibly disables the lasers. This reveals glass display cases holding ancient artifacts, including a large black cat statue and a golden headdress. The Woman with dark skin then stands with a Red-haired man in a black suit, discussing their plan. She confidently declares, "It's good to be me," to which the man retorts, "Took you long enough." She explains she "had to wait for someone to lure the guard away" and urges, "Let's just get this done." With a distinct sound of a glass case opening and a delicate clink, she picks up a Golden necklace. Suddenly, a Woman with denim jacket and glasses, now in a blue jacket and yellow-framed glasses, appears, looking shocked and alarmed as she witnesses the theft. The museum's lighting shifts to a deep red, heightening the tension. A young female voice calls out, "Hey, Dad. Mom," prompting the Woman with dark skin to sternly remind her, "We told you to wait in the car! And to use our supervillain names when we're in public!" The young woman, now identified as the daughter, resignedly whispers, "Fine. Invisibility," as the thieves prepare to escape.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit museum hall at night, the Woman with dark skin, identified as Leo, the Shocktress, wearing a black sleeveless top, triangular earrings, and a choker, confronts the Red-haired man in a black suit, who gestures defensively. Leo, with a confident and slightly dismissive tone, critiques power names, while the Red-haired man, sounding exasperated, complains about the difficulty of finding unique supervillain names. The Woman with dark skin then turns to the Woman with denim jacket and glasses, who stands with arms crossed, wearing yellow-framed glasses and a blue jacket over a yellow top, listening intently. The Woman with denim jacket, in an assertive and scolding voice, criticizes Leo's attitude and denies any family connection to a lack of powers, later adding, "Hey, hey, hey! There's no need for that tone, young lady. We do and do and do for you, and we getting no respect. Now go on! Stay out the way." Leo, in response, defensively states she was "Just trying to help" and reminds them she "told you about this exhibit in the first place." As Leo points emphatically, her expression shifting from confident to focused, the Red-haired man warns, "Just step back." The scene shifts to a display case with red laser grids, securing a golden headdress. A tense whirring and crackling sound builds, accompanied by a high-pitched whine. The Woman with dark skin channels blue energy from her hands, disabling the red laser grids with a loud burst of light and electrical crackling, followed by a distinct thump and metallic clang. She then approaches the case, retrieves the golden headdress, and holds it triumphantly. The Woman with denim jacket reacts with a shocked scream, raising her hands in alarm, and analytically observes, "They must have a localized micro-generator." The Red-haired man watches with a stern expression, dramatically exclaiming, "Oh, my life is so hard!" as Leo begins to speak again, "Maybe if you..." before being cut off by building ominous synth sounds and a low hum. The atmosphere remains tense, with the museum’s starry cityscape visible through large windows, and the lighting shifts to a dramatic red hue during the theft, emphasizing the high-stakes nature of the heist, culminating in another scream from the Woman with denim jacket.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit museum hall, where the Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top, wearing triangular earrings and a choker, smirks confidently while holding a microphone. She gestures dismissively, telling someone in a confident female voice, "Just go with. Go. You know, go drink a smoothie, huh? That's something that powerless people do. You go do that. You think of something!" The Red-haired man in a black suit stands beside her, observing with a stern expression, while the Woman with denim jacket and glasses, wearing yellow-framed glasses and a blue jacket, looks on nervously, clutching her wrist. As the Woman with dark skin raises her fingers in a precise motion, seemingly disabling the red laser grids that crisscross the room, the Red-haired man begins to explain in a male voice, "If the lasers aren't reading the full atomic spectrum..." His explanation is cut short by a tense, building musical score. With a loud "Whoa!" sound effect, the lasers deactivate, and the Woman with dark skin swiftly grabs the Golden headdress from its glass case, her expression shifting to focused determination. The Red-haired man, surprised, exclaims, "Oh, they are! They definitely are!" A "What?!" sound, followed by a loud "CRASH!" and the Red-haired man stating, "Reinforced glass!" indicates a struggle. The Woman with dark skin taunts, "You got a blowtorch for your last birthday? I don't suppose you remembered to bring it?" to which the Red-haired man retorts, "Why would I think I needed a blowtorch? Just leave the crown." He then sarcastically adds, "Also, please add hammer to my next birthday wish list," as a dramatic, slightly mischievous musical score builds with accompanying "Ha!", "Whoa!", "CRASH!", and "Whoosh!" sound effects. The music continues with a sustained, almost magical sound. The scene then cuts to a park at night, where the Boy with reddish-brown hair, now wearing a black jacket over a red hoodie and a silver pendant, stands alone on a path, looking downcast. The dramatic museum music fades, replaced by a somber, reflective musical theme. He turns his head slowly, his expression somber, as a male voice (likely his own) says, "You want to see me? Okay, I'm here." The camera pans to reveal a woman in a teal jacket and pink scarf walking away from him, her back to the camera, suggesting a moment of emotional distance or separation.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a somber, dramatic musical score as Dante, a Boy with reddish-brown hair, stands alone in a Park at night, his expression distant as he states, "But this is a bad idea." Gabriella, a Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, approaches him, gently placing a comforting hand on his shoulder. She calls out "Dante!" and he responds, "Gabriella," asking, "How did you find me?" Gabriella, wearing a teal shirt, glasses, and a teardrop pendant, earnestly explains, "I used a private detective. I'm here to bring you home." Dante, showing concern, asks if his parents know and are "okay with it," to which Gabriella replies, "Not yet, but they will be." His face showing hesitation, Dante declares, "Then I won't go back." Gabriella urges him to "Give them time, they'll understand?" but Dante, with an intense tone, insists, "They already understand. I am dangerous. What happened before? I can't hurt them again." Gabriella then questions, "What about everyone here? You're not worried about them?" Dante explains, "I'm around people like me, okay? We help each other." Gabriella pleads, "You already have a family that loves you. Don't give up on that." With a pleading tone, Dante tells her, "Gabby, please, just go home." He then turns away, walking slowly down the path, leaving Gabriella standing alone, her expression one of worry and frustration as the music fades. The scene abruptly cuts to a brightly lit Music Store with warm, vibrant lighting. A Man with a mustache, wearing a gray suit, smiles as he handles a golden necklace with red gemstones, placing it into a glass display case, while a male voice (likely his) confidently states, "I'll start contacting buyers. This stuff must be worth millions." The camera pans to reveal Dante, now seated on a blue couch holding a green vase, alongside a Woman with dark skin and a black sleeveless top, who stands confidently holding a chain. Her assertive voice is heard saying, "Even though we didn't get the crown because someone got in the way." A Woman with a denim jacket and glasses, initially reading a book, looks up with a surprised and alarmed expression, her voice defensively asking, "I didn't stop you from getting the crown? I..." The cozy, colorful store, filled with musical instruments and records, provides a sharp contrast to the earlier emotional park scene.

Gabby, a girl with blonde hair and pink tips, appears concerned as she speaks to a serious-looking boy with reddish-brown hair. He sternly asks, "Why are you here, Gabby?", to which she firmly replies, "I'm not going back to Illinois without you. I mean it." The boy, intense and frustrated, retorts, "And I meant everything that I said last night. You admitted it. Mom and Dad aren't ready to forgive me." Gabby softly pleads, "Give them time," but he sharply insists, "Time won't fix this. You shouldn't have come here." With a quiet, resigned tone, he tells her, "Just... go." A wide shot shows them walking away from the fire with the cityscape in the background. The scene then transitions to a brightly lit kitchen, accompanied by cheerful, lighthearted music. A woman with dark skin smiles as she stirs a large pot of vibrant orange stew. Gabby, now delighted, tastes the stew, exclaiming, "Mmm, chicken soup. I'm so hungry right now." The woman calmly warns, "I wouldn't," just as a small, squirrel-like creature with a yellow collar and silver medallion appears, making distinct squeaking and chittering sounds as it mischievously steals a spoonful of stew. Whimsical, mischievous music plays as the final shot reveals a large bag of dog food labeled 'YUM!' with a husky on the front, confirming the stew's true purpose, leading Gabby to exclaim, "Ew."

segments.integrated_caption: In a dimly lit urban alley at dusk, amidst red brick buildings and graffiti-covered walls, a tense confrontation unfolds. It feels simple at first and then suddenly it's like a living thing." But she dismisses him sarcastically, "Oh, was it super hard for you? Thanks for the advice, but I got this!" as she continues to wield the fire, singing confidently, "On the edge, revolution, my rules, my rules..." The alley, littered with trash and crates, and the ominous red-orange sky underscore the power imbalance and the Boy’s vulnerability against the Woman’s supernatural abilities, which she now seems to control with ease.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dimly lit alley behind Wilson's Auto Shop, where the Girl with blonde hair and pink tips sits visibly distressed amidst erupting flames. The Boy with reddish-brown hair rushes to her side, urgently calling out, "Secret Warriors? I've got a situation here." The Girl asks, "What happened?" to which the Boy replies, "This is why you shouldn't be around me." She defiantly states, "I'm not leaving you," and he urges, "Then you have to at least get back." Upbeat pop music with female vocals plays as the Woman with denim jacket and glasses, her golden necklace with turquoise beads glowing intensely, appears and unleashes fiery energy from her hands, striking the Boy and throwing him back in pain. The Woman then turns her attention to the terrified Girl. The scene expands to a city street now engulfed in flames, with the Woman continuing her fiery assault. The Boy struggles to recover, his face contorted in pain and fear, later asking, "What's going on?" and explaining, "It was Zayla, the new girl from the museum. She took my powers somehow. She's wearing this necklace, which she got away." Amidst the chaos, the Girl with long black hair, now in a superhero costume with a red scarf and black mask, holds a Pink Smartphone, seemingly trying to call for help, exclaiming, "Wait, no, that wasn't... Oh no, your hazelnut! Help's almost there!" The Woman with denim jacket and glasses confidently retorts, "I don't need help! I've got everything I need!" followed by an explosion. The Superhero Girl then runs through the flames with determination, shouting, "Keep moving everyone, nothing to see here! Except maybe a girl who's only got seven minutes to make it home for dinner!" The Woman with dark skin, now in a superhero outfit with a star emblem, also runs through the fire with urgency, declaring, "We're gonna need more than this!" The segment concludes with the Boy and Girl huddled together, both looking terrified as the flames rage around them, accompanied by the persistent pop music and sounds of fire and explosions.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a fiery urban environment, a building engulfed in flames, accompanied by the crackling and roar of fire and explosions. The Superhero Girl with red scarf and black mask is seen rescuing a civilian girl with reddish-brown hair and glasses from the burning building, as a taunting female voice, later identified as the Antagonist, asks, "Looking for a lift?". The Boy with reddish-brown hair assists in the rescue, helping the civilian girl down a fire escape amidst the chaos. As they reach safety, the Superhero Girl turns to confront the Antagonist woman in a white and black suit, who appears menacing. A driving rock music track kicks in as the two engage in a dynamic fight atop the rooftops, with sounds of combat and whooshes. The Boy's voice is heard asking, "These are the people you were telling me about?", to which the Superhero Girl's voice responds, "We're not just people, we're his team." She then asks the Antagonist, "Also, who are you?". The Antagonist, with a playful yet menacing tone, exclaims, "Hey America, Ghost Spider! Let's make it right!" and later adds, "Hope you're thirsty, one giant drink of water on the way!", followed by a splash. The battle intensifies with more explosions and whooshes as the Superhero Girl is struck by fire, falls, but recovers and continues fighting. The Boy's voice, sounding impatient, asks, "Can we please wrap this up in like no minutes? Seriously? This again?", to which the Superhero Girl's voice replies, "I'll give you a ride home if you stop talking about dinner." The Boy quickly agrees, "Deal." The Antagonist then announces, "Everyone stand clear if you don't want a major shower!", followed by a large splash/explosion. A wider view shows the cityscape's destruction, with the Boy and civilian girl looking up in concern. The Superhero Girl, determined, prepares to strike back, and the fight continues with both combatants using their powers, creating explosions and debris. The scene ends with the Superhero Girl standing triumphantly, having defeated the Antagonist, while the Boy and civilian girl look on in relief, the environment filled with smoke and fire, and the city skyline illuminated by the orange glow of the flames, as the rock music continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene unfolds on a city street at dusk, bathed in warm orange and red light, where a tense conversation is underway among four young individuals. The Civilian girl, Gabriella, with reddish-brown hair and glasses, initially speaks urgently, gesturing with her hands, her voice rambling and self-correcting as she awkwardly mentions the fire being out and the alley looking cleaner, adding a nervous 'I'll stop, I'll stop.' The Boy with reddish-brown hair stands concerned beside her. The Superhero Girl, with a red scarf and black mask, interjects with a serious tone, stating, 'But what's her name got away with Dante's powers,' before her expression shifts to surprise and her voice to awe, exclaiming, 'That was amazing! Now I know what you meant when you said your friends have your back.' The Boy then calmly introduces Gabriella as 'my sister,' who 'just got to town.' Both the Superhero Girl and the Superhero Woman, with a star emblem, react with overlapping exclamations of 'Your sister!' in surprise, with the Superhero Woman adding an enthusiastic 'So good to meet you.' The Boy, slightly awkward, suggests, 'Come on. Let's give them some time to talk.' The Civilian girl removes her glasses, revealing a more confident demeanor, while the Superhero Girl's expression turns to determination as she pulls out a Pink Smartphone. Her voice becomes resolute, declaring, 'We really need to track down Zayla before she burns down all of Jersey.' Gabriella, now putting her glasses back on, enthusiastically responds, 'Oh, I'm on it yesterday!' but quickly shifts to a frustrated 'Uh, I'm so grounded!' The Superhero Woman makes a questioning 'Huh?' sound. The group's attention briefly turns to the menacing Antagonist woman, Zayla, in a white and black suit, her mask glowing faintly. The Superhero Girl and Superhero Woman then mount a red motorcycle, donning helmets and goggles, as its engine revs loudly. The Superhero Girl confidently states, 'See you back at HQ later,' as they prepare to ride away, leaving behind urban buildings and a fiery orange sky, hinting at an impending crisis.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with the Civilian girl, Ami, with reddish-brown hair and glasses, asking "Right?", while the Boy with reddish-brown hair, looking concerned, responds with an "Mhm." The camera pulls back to an aerial view of a quiet suburban neighborhood at dusk, as a red motorcycle with a star emblem on the front wheel revs loudly, speeds down the street, and screeches to an abrupt halt. The rider, the Superhero Woman, America Chavez, in her blue and white superhero outfit, helmet, and goggles, dismounts and confronts the Woman in a pink hoodie and glasses, who, startled, expresses gratitude, "Thank you so much. I owe you one." America Chavez, now without her helmet, firmly replies, "Don't mention it. I mean that. Let's please not go over it again." The Woman in the pink hoodie, later revealed as Ami's mother, exclaims "Gommalakhan!" in surprise, then suspiciously questions, "Do you mean to tell me you rode on that?" America Chavez defends herself, stating, "I wore a helmet!" As Ami, holding a blue bowl and spoon, joins them looking anxious, Ami's mother asks, "And who is this?" Ami introduces herself, "Uh, I'm Ami," and then her friend, "This is my friend America Chavez," to which America Chavez politely adds, "Nice to meet you." With the motorcycle idling in the background, Ami's mother, now smiling slightly, assertively declares, "Hold on. You are staying for dinner," cutting off America Chavez's attempts to decline. She explains, "If someone is driving my daughter around on a dangerous machine of death, I want to get to know them. There's plenty of food," before insisting, "No buts. Now come inside." Ami, her expression shifting from worry to alertness, questions, "I thought you said this is a special family celebration?" Her mother confirms, "It is. We're celebrating you." The scene concludes with Ami's mother turning away, hand on her hip, as America Chavez and Ami exchange a glance, a decision made under the warm glow of streetlights in the calm neighborhood.

Something that strong must have some unique properties.” The Red-haired woman, pointing at the tablet, then adds, “Yeah, but I couldn't find any information on it at all. Like, none,” as the group appears focused and concerned in the high-tech, modern environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a surveillance-style view labeled 'cam 01', showing a burning building with a figure running away, suggesting an emergency. A female voice, belonging to the Red-haired woman in a denim jacket, explains, "Oh, I switched tactics and found this. Security footage from all over town. She's definitely been busy." The scene quickly shifts to a high-tech, futuristic recreational space where the Red-haired woman, a Girl with long black hair, a Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, and a Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top are intently watching a large screen displaying the burning scene. The Red-haired woman holds a tablet, pointing at it as she explains. The Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, with a higher-pitched voice, interjects, "Uh, did she burn down an ice cream cart? Who does that?" The Boy with reddish-brown hair joins them, his expression tense, and he and the Girl with long black hair exchange a worried glance. The Boy, identified as "Dante" by the Girl with long black hair who sarcastically remarks, "Been working on your silent lurking, I see," states in a calm voice, "She's gotten better at controlling it... She's more precise. Looks more confident." The Red-haired woman warns, "Which is when she'll get reckless. And dangerous," then adds, "We'll figure this out. There has to be a way to get your powers back." The Girl with blonde hair asks, "Hey, where's Gabriella?" to which the Woman with dark skin offers, "Your sister should stay here if she wants. There's definitely space." Dante thanks "Gwen" (the Woman with dark skin) and clarifies, "She's at a hotel." The Girl with blonde hair excitedly suggests, "Tell her to come over! Not gonna miss a chance to quiz her about your super awkward pre-teen years." The Girl with long black hair expresses surprise, "We didn't even know you were in touch with your family," and Dante hesitantly replies, "I'm not. Or, I wasn't. She tracked me down." The environment then shifts dramatically to a whimsical, absurd setting, cutting to a close-up of a large, sleeping creature resembling a giant hamburger with a face, drooling green goo and emitting gurgling snores. A small squirrel-like creature with a yellow collar makes a squeak as it mischievously steals a spoonful of stew from a steaming pot beside the giant hamburger, creating a stark comedic contrast to the earlier tension.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a vibrant, futuristic recreational space, where a large, anthropomorphic giant hamburger creature sleeps soundly, green goo dripping from its mouth. A small, brown, squirrel-like creature with a yellow collar, named Tips, is heard chittering and slurping as it mischievously steals a spoonful of stew from a steaming pot beside the sleeping creature, then sits smugly on a blue mug. The giant hamburger creature lets out a deep, guttural growl and squishy sounds, followed by more chittering from Tips. The camera shifts to Kamala, a red-haired woman in a denim jacket, who smiles confidently and addresses the squirrel, saying, "Ah, Tips, you're a natural healer." Kamala then turns to a woman with long dark hair, wearing a red jacket with star emblems and a star belt buckle, who stands with hands on hips and asks, "Hey, Kamala. Dinner at 7, right? Your mom asked me to pick up some chapati." Kamala replies humorously, "I'll do it. Once I figure out what that is," before saying, "Yeah, America? See you later," to someone off-screen. Confused, Kamala asks, "What the what was that about?" Kamala's friend explains, "When she dropped me off yesterday, Ummi invited her for dinner and she said yes. And then Ummi invited her for dinner again tonight and she said yes again." Kamala then turns to a woman with long dark hair in a teal jacket and pink scarf, who gestures as she speaks, asking with a slightly hesitant tone, "So she, like, had fun?" Kamala responds with uncertainty, "I guess? I wouldn't have expected her to, but..." The scene expands to include a girl with blonde hair and pink tips in a white off-shoulder top and a boy with reddish-brown hair in a black jacket over a red hoodie, both looking concerned. The boy, with a calm voice, suggests, "Well, you said your mom's a good cook, so maybe she really liked the food." Suddenly, a blaring siren alarm cuts through the air. The group gathers around Kamala, who holds a tablet displaying a red 'ALERT!' message with a yellow triangle, indicating an urgent situation. Kamala's friend urgently exclaims, "Fire in a shopping center!" The high-tech environment, with its climbing wall and large windows showing mountains, emphasizes the group’s focused and serious mood as they prepare for action.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a futuristic recreational space with a high-tech, multi-level design. Three young women—the Red-haired woman in a denim jacket, the Girl with blonde hair and pink tips, and the Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses—stand together, engaged in a serious conversation. A female voice bets "a billion Brazil nuts at Zale," followed by the enthusiastic voice of the Girl with blonde hair and pink tips exclaiming, "Good thinking, Doreen! Let's go!", identifying the Woman with long dark hair and yellow glasses as Doreen. The Red-haired woman gestures animatedly as she speaks, while the others listen intently. The scene then cuts to the Boy with reddish-brown hair, who looks concerned and turns his head sharply, reacting to something off-screen. His dejected male voice states he "wouldn't be much help," but the Red-haired woman's voice encourages him to "stay here and monitor things" and "send us updates," emphasizing, "We always need updates." The Girl with blonde hair and pink tips enthusiastically reinforces, "Yes, every team has someone in a chair... You're that someone in the chair!" The Boy with reddish-brown hair, with a resigned male voice, accepts, "Guy in the chair. Right." A vehicle then starts and revs loudly. The setting shifts to a city street with colorful buildings, where a fire truck is parked near a damaged storefront. The Red-haired woman's urgent voice declares, "It's around the back. We got her now, let's go!" followed by a gasp. She then observes, "Looks like a big fat natta on the arsenal alert," which the Girl with blonde hair and pink tips dismisses as "probably just a fry cook who let things get out of control." The Red-haired woman skeptically questions, "Does it though?" before an affirmative "Mhm." The Girl with blonde hair and pink tips then casually asks about dinner plans, "Hey, it's almost dinner time. Meet at your house? I gotta pick up that chapati bread thing," to which Doreen agrees, "Yep, great." The sound of a powerful engine revving fills the air. The group, now in superhero costumes—the Superhero Girl with red scarf and black mask, and the Superhero Woman with star emblem—confront each other with the Antagonist woman in a white and black suit. The Superhero Girl points accusingly at the Antagonist, who stands with arms crossed, while the Superhero Woman observes with a stern expression. The Superhero Girl's exasperated voice asks, "When did my life get so weird? I mean, besides the stretchy thing," before playfully demanding, "I'm gonna need a full dinner report with diagrams and footnotes. Also some..." The group then moves down the sidewalk, with the Superhero Girl leading and the others following. The scene ends with the group standing together, the Superhero Girl raising her fist in determination, as the Antagonist woman glares back, creating a tense standoff.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a warmly lit dining room at night, where Ummi, wearing a pink hijab and glasses, sits with her family around an oval table. Soft background music plays as Ummi, in a warm, appreciative voice, thanks America, who is wearing a denim jacket, red scarf, and yellow top, for bringing the chapati. America, appearing concerned, asks if she "got the right kind," to which Ummi responds "Perfect," offering to teach her how to make dal. America, pleased, accepts. Kamala's father, the man with a mustache in a white shirt, approves with a deep "So helpful." Ummi then playfully teases Kamala, who is in a red jacket with star emblems, about her "crafty craft" activities. Kamala, with a surprised and annoyed expression, holding a fork over her plate, retorts, "Ummi, I told you, it's not... never mind." America then asks about Amir, and Kamala's father, with a stern expression and spoon paused mid-air, informs them that "Your brother is volunteering at the soup kitchen tonight." Ummi, however, quickly adds, "But we're glad you're here," before turning curious, asking the girls how they met and if America is in Kamala's school. America corrects her, stating, "No, actually, we're on a team." Kamala, now animated and excited, jumps in to explain, "The bowlers! Where we bowl and then get donuts and talk about the bowling team while we eat donuts. Like we did that one time!" America confirms with a "Right. Bowling" and a small giggle. Kamala's father, looking sternly, addresses Kamala with disbelief, "You, my daughter, you bowl?" as Ummi calmly, with a slight smile, attempts to interject with "Oh, oh, on...", while America places a reassuring hand on Kamala's shoulder, who looks down, appearing conflicted.
segments.visual_original_caption: The scene opens with a wide shot of a warmly lit dining room at night, where a family of four is seated around a large oval table covered with a white tablecloth, enjoying a meal together. The room features teal walls, red curtains, framed artwork, and a potted plant, creating a cozy domestic atmosphere. The family consists of a man with gray hair and a mustache in a white shirt, a woman in a pink hijab and glasses, and two younger women — one in a red jacket with star emblems and a black top, the other in a denim jacket with a red scarf and yellow top. As the camera zooms in, the younger woman in the denim jacket appears concerned and speaks animatedly, gesturing with her hands while the woman in the red jacket listens with a mix of surprise and annoyance. The woman in the pink hijab turns to listen, her expression shifting from neutral to slightly amused, then to thoughtful. The man in the white shirt looks on with a stern expression, his spoon paused mid-air, suggesting tension or disapproval. The younger woman in the denim jacket places a reassuring hand on the shoulder of the woman in the red jacket, who then looks down, appearing conflicted. The scene ends with the man in the white shirt looking sternly at the woman in the pink hijab, who responds with a calm, slightly smiling expression, indicating a possible resolution or understanding.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a warmly lit living room, adorned with abstract art and cozy furnishings, including a blue couch and hanging lamps. A female voice, assertive and slightly exasperated, declares, "Bam! See that? It's too much traffic past the graveyard." The Red-haired man in black suit, initially smug and confident, responds with a dismissive laugh, "That's because people are dying to get in there," before conceding, "You're right, we'll do the tunnel." His expression quickly shifts to alarm as the Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top confronts him with a stern, assertive posture, hands on her hips, adjusting her large triangular earring. She speaks in an overly nurturing yet patronizing tone, stating, "Sweetheart, I mean this in the most nurturing and maternal way. And we want you to do you. But there's nothing you can help us with," followed by an emphatic, "Really? Nothing!" As the tension escalates, the Woman with denim jacket and glasses enters from a doorway, her golden necklace with turquoise beads glowing as she conjures a small flame in her palm. She enthusiastically exclaims, "Mom, Dad, I have something I really want to show you!" and "This is really cool. If you just check it out and see what I can do and then maybe I can help you." The Red-haired man, with a dismissive and sarcastic male voice, cuts her off, "Now, now, sweetie, Mommy and Daddy are doing important supervillain things," and later, "We love you anyway, blah blah blah, you're perfect the way you are." The Woman with denim jacket and glasses, now frustrated, pleads, "Will you please just listen to me for once!" The Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top, maintaining her dominance, points accusingly at him and sternly reprimands, "Oh, not with that tone, young lady. Now go to your room!" The Red-haired man reacts with shock and defensive gestures to the flame. The Woman with denim jacket and glasses steps back, her expression shifting from focused to surprised, observing the confrontation with concern as her flame flickers. The Woman with dark skin and black sleeveless top, exasperated, mutters, "Ugh... I'll tell you the one power I wish she had. Being seen and not heard," to which the Red-haired man dismissively agrees, "Okay." The atmosphere is charged with conflict, suggesting a power struggle or revelation, with the glowing necklace and flame hinting at supernatural or technological elements at play.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a warmly lit living room with abstract paintings and wooden furniture, where a woman with dark skin and a black sleeveless top confronts a red-haired man in a black suit. He appears smug, uttering a dismissive "my pet, boom," before becoming defensive as a woman with curly hair, yellow-framed glasses, and a golden necklace with turquoise beads places her hand on a wooden shutter. Her necklace glows as she ignites the shutter with a glowing orange flame, confidently declaring, "I'll get the one thing you couldn't. Then everyone will see me and hear me, loud and clear!" The red-haired man reacts with alarm as the fire spreads, accompanied by the sound of crackling flames, a thoughtful "Hmm," and an exclamation of "Woo!" Amidst the chaos, an unidentified male voice casually suggests, "How about grabbing dinner when we finish? I am craving barbecue." The scene cuts to a high-tech, futuristic recreational space where a red-haired woman in a denim jacket and brown hat, accompanied by a squeaking squirrel-like creature named Tap on her shoulder, views surveillance footage on a laptop. The footage shows the living room incident, including the woman with the golden necklace using her powers. The red-haired woman reacts with concern, explaining her technical work: "I tweaked my facial recognition program to overlay onto social media feeds. We'll find her. I'd have to pat myself on the back, mostly because my arms are kind of on the short side." A girl with long black hair in a denim jacket and red scarf joins her, looking serious, while an unidentified male voice thoughtfully hums. A boy with reddish-brown hair in a black hoodie with a red flame emblem appears, pointing accusingly at the girl, who looks surprised. Sounds of punching and grunting are heard, followed by the boy exclaiming, "Punching bag! Nice. Very Captain America." The setting shifts to a wide shot of the futuristic space, showing a circular platform and climbing wall, as the group prepares for action. The red-haired woman gestures toward the platform, and the boy with reddish-brown hair points toward it, indicating a plan. The girl with long black hair looks concerned, and the boy’s expression turns intense as he clenches his fists, signaling readiness for confrontation, expressing his frustration: "I hate that I can't help when it's my powers causing the trouble." The girl reassures him, "Last I checked, you're not the one blowing up ice cream carts. And you are helping. As much as any of us right now."

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens on a vibrant city street with colorful buildings, including one with a 'FURNITURE' sign, under a bright blue sky. The Superhero Girl, wearing a red scarf and black mask, stands on the sidewalk, gesturing with concern as she asks, "To hear them ask about my life, in my friends?" A distorted, robotic voice interjects, "Fresh hot low-grade data, coming in now." The Superhero Girl then apologizes, "I'm... I'm sorry, I guess I didn't realize that..." beside the Superhero Woman with a star emblem, who stands with arms crossed. The Superhero Woman then points accusingly with a red-gloved hand, her expression turning furious as she angrily retorts, "That you take everything you have for granted? Or that I might miss the people who loved me?" The Superhero Girl tries to interject, "No, it's just...", but the Superhero Woman cuts her off, declaring, "Let's just go deal with it." She then dons a red and blue helmet with goggles and mounts a red motorcycle with a star emblem, which revs loudly and speeds off with a powerful engine roar, eliciting a gasp from the Superhero Girl. The scene shifts to a museum hall with a tense, dramatic score. A man in a gray suit angrily confronts a civilian girl with reddish-brown hair and glasses, who looks alarmed. Suddenly, the Woman with a denim jacket and glasses appears, conjuring a small flame in her palm as her golden necklace with turquoise beads glows. With a loud glass-shattering sound, she uses her powers to break through a display case, revealing a golden headdress with a snake motif. Magical whooshing and thumping sounds accompany her as she puts on the headdress, transforming into a powerful figure with glowing eyes and a confident stance. A confident female voice, likely hers, remarks, "I don't know why my parents had to make such a big production of this," followed by "See, Dad?" and "What do you think of that, Mom?" The scene cuts back to the museum, where the Superhero Girl and Superhero Woman are now joined by the Antagonist woman in a white and black suit, who stands silently with a menacing posture, her mask glowing faintly. The Woman with the denim jacket and glasses, now transformed, stands confidently, her expression calm and determined, as the museum’s lighting shifts to a dramatic red hue, accompanied by more magical sounds. A sarcastic female voice, belonging to the Antagonist, comments, "That's not really your look. I'm thinking more orange jumpsuit. Maybe some handcuffs." The transformed Woman defiantly responds, "So sorry you couldn't catch me last time, but..." as the audio fades.

segments.integrated_caption: In a dark, high-tech museum hall, the Woman with denim jacket and glasses, wearing a blue jacket over a yellow top and yellow-framed glasses, stands confidently, her golden necklace glowing. She declares, "looking at me again, huge mistake," as the Superhero Girl with red scarf and black mask, in her blue and yellow costume, retorts, "you're the one who made the mistake lighting things up all over town," proudly adding, "My genius facial recognition tracker picked up a 100% hit." The Superhero Woman with star emblem, in her blue and white outfit, skeptically asks, "Since when are squirrels tech geniuses?" to which the Superhero Girl corrects, "that's Squirrel Girl. The genius comes from the girl part," then whispers, "No one tell Tippy I said that." With a confident smile, the Woman with denim jacket exclaims, "But you can't tell her I did this!" and gestures, conjuring a small flame. A whoosh and explosion erupt as she ignites a nearby object, causing a burst of fire and debris. The Superhero Girl quickly raises her hand to shield herself, while the Antagonist woman in white and black suit, with her white hood and red spiderweb patterns, remains still, her mask glowing faintly amidst the chaos. The Woman with denim jacket, seemingly pleased, remarks, "Looks like I've gotten pretty good at this, huh?" The Superhero Woman with star emblem steps forward, her expression stern, pointing accusingly and stating, "You took something that's not yours. Really should have learned that lesson in preschool." Suddenly, with sounds of energy charging and whooshing, the Woman with denim jacket places a golden headdress on her head, triggering a transformation. The museum lighting shifts to a dramatic red. Surprised, she denies, "Wait, I- I didn't do that. And I really didn't do that." The Superhero Woman with star emblem explains, "You touched her, and there was this red glowy thing that like flew out of you, and now there's a new bead glowing red." The group, including the transformed Woman with denim jacket, stands together amidst the glass display cases and ancient artifacts, ready for the next phase of their high-stakes confrontation.

The woman with glasses then declares with increasing power, "Anyway, the point is, I don't want just some powers. I want them all!" accompanied by a powerful magical surge. She menacingly adds, "Wait! This is way too easy. Think I'm in the mood for a bonfire instead," as a superhero girl in a black and red costume raises her hand to shield herself from intense magical blasts and explosions, while an urgent voice cries, "We need cover!"

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a grand museum hall with large windows overlooking a cityscape. A woman with curly brown hair, wearing a blue jacket over an orange top and yellow-framed glasses, appears startled and defensive, clutching her chest. A male voice is heard over a comm, stating he couldn't leave his team, followed by a distressed female voice echoing, "I couldn't leave my team." An assertive female voice then retorts, "Grow up!" The woman is soon joined by a girl with reddish-brown hair and green eyes, wearing a green and brown outfit with a large orange backpack, who holds a small squirrel-like creature with a yellow collar and silver medallion. The girl looks concerned as the creature, named Tippy, suddenly transforms into a fiery, glowing squirrel, causing chaos with a burst of flames and an explosion, accompanied by grunts and exclamations of surprise. Tippy emits frantic squeaking and chittering sounds. The girl cries out in panic, "No, no, no, no, no, no, Tippy, I can't understand you! This can't be happening!" as Tippy continues its frantic, unintelligible squeaking. The girl exclaims, "I don't know what you're saying!" A menacing female voice, belonging to the antagonist in the white and black suit, taunts, "Oh, you've seen stuff you just can't unsee, haven't you, squirrel?" The woman with the glasses reacts with surprise as the creature bursts into flames, sending sparks flying. The girl, now visibly distressed, apologizes, "I'm so sorry! She took my powers! I don't know what to do!" The antagonist woman, with a determined and sarcastic tone, declares, "We'll try to unsee this!" The scene shifts to show a superhero girl in a blue and yellow costume with a red scarf and black mask, standing beside a man with reddish-brown hair in a black hoodie with a red flame emblem. They are joined by a woman in a blue jacket with a white star emblem and red gloves, and another woman in a white and black suit with red spiderweb patterns and a white hood (the antagonist). The group appears alarmed as the fiery squirrel creature continues to cause destruction, shattering glass and sending debris flying, accompanied by loud explosions and the shattering of glass. Grunts and sounds of impact indicate the heroes are being affected. The antagonist woman triumphantly exclaims, "It is good to be me again." The man with reddish-brown hair, surprised, states, "You got your powers back!" The superhero girl with the red scarf urgently explains, "It happened after the red bead was knocked off the necklace!" The woman with the glasses is knocked to the ground, clutching her golden necklace with turquoise beads, which glows faintly. The man with reddish-brown hair, now determined, instructs, "So get the beads off the necklace, get our powers back!" The superhero girl raises her hand in defense as the chaos intensifies, while the woman in the star-emblazoned outfit points accusingly, her expression turning furious. The superhero woman with the star emblem calmly adds, "Which means getting close enough to grab them." The superhero girl confidently declares, "No problem!" Upbeat, slightly ominous background music begins to play, with an exclamation of "Whoa!" amidst the music. The environment shifts from calm to chaotic, with shattered glass, debris, and fire illuminating the once-peaceful museum hall under dramatic red lighting. The scene is filled with sounds of impact, struggle, and grunts as the action intensifies, concluding with a final exclamation of "Dude!"

She then commands, "All right, troops, get the bead off that necklace!" as the sounds of multiplying squirrels and destruction escalate. The scene culminates in a dramatic explosion of fire and smoke, with the museum hall engulfed in chaos, the banner 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS' partially obscured. Amidst the destruction, the girl with the backpack stands defiantly, her expression shifting from shock to resolve, triumphantly stating, "Another one down! Ah! The numbers are not looking good for you." The background music continues with the male vocalist singing, "Don't care if the world ain't really for me," culminating in a final powerful explosion.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a massive explosion in a grand museum hall, sending debris flying and engulfing the area in roaring flames, accompanied by intense whooshing and crackling sounds. Amidst the chaos, the Woman with denim jacket and glasses is knocked to the ground with a shocked grunt. As she rises, her voice is heard urgently stating, "I know you're not all in on this power thing? I overheard you and your sister, so just walk away and we're cool," attempting to de-escalate the situation. However, the Boy with reddish-brown hair, in a black hoodie, rushes into the burning hall with a determined expression, responding with a resolute male voice, "Not a chance." He retrieves a large, glowing ornate scythe from a shattered glass display case. An unseen female antagonist's voice then mocks, "Powers like these should only go to someone who really wants them," to which the Boy retorts, "I don't just want powers to want them. They're part of me. Another part of me is someone who helps his friends, no matter what." The classical museum hall, with its tall columns and red carpeting, is now a battleground of fire and rubble. The Boy uses his glowing scythe to deflect a fiery blast, accompanied by loud impacts and whooshing. The antagonist's voice taunts, "Swords? Against a superpower? That's cute. But I'm not playing around," followed by a powerful "AHHH!" and a massive energy blast. The Boy grunts with effort as he continues to fight. The antagonist boasts, "I'm more powerful than you can ever be! Anyone can ever be!" but the Boy challenges back, "You're not powerful just because you can make fire or have super strength. It's how you use it." The antagonist defiantly asks, "How about like this?" before unleashing another powerful "AHHH!" and a building energy attack, as the Woman's golden necklace with turquoise beads pulses with light. The scene concludes with the Boy standing resolute, scythe in hand, as flames rage around him, preparing for the escalating confrontation amidst the intense sounds of battle.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a well-lit museum hall, adorned with a red carpet and glass display cases, under a banner proclaiming 'TREASURES OF THE ANCIENTS'. A large, orange dog, wearing a golden feathered headdress with a red gem, lies dazed or injured on the floor, emitting soft yelps and whines after a magical whoosh sound. The Antagonist woman, in a white and black suit with red spiderweb patterns, taunts the dog, calling the headdress a 'crown' and sarcastically remarking, "It looks so good on you, mush face! And you are royalty. But I think it's gotta go back." The Superhero Girl, with a red scarf and black mask, approaches the dog with concern, then turns to the Man with reddish-brown hair in a black hoodie with a red flame emblem. The Man, gesturing seriously, reflects aloud, "Must feel good to have your powers back... I used to hate what my powers turned me into... But what they really did is make me me." The Superhero Girl, listening intently, questions him, "You were wearing your inferno costume under your clothes the whole time?" to which he replies, "I just really couldn't leave my team." As the camera pans to show the group, including the stern-faced Superhero Woman with a star emblem and the neutral-looking Girl with reddish-brown hair and green eyes, the Superhero Girl places a hand on the Man’s arm and adjusts her mask thoughtfully. She then asks about his family, and he responds about being patient and finding "a new way forward." She concludes, "Even without powers, we seriously pull together like family today. America, you were right. I know Ami is on me all the time because she wants the best for me." The environment remains calm, with large windows showing a cityscape, suggesting a daytime setting, as the group discusses the incident.
